```{r select-best-combinations}
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "striped", full_width = FALSE)

best_2_dv <- dv_avg$DataVersion[1:2]
cat("\nBest 2 data versions:", paste(best_2_dv, collapse = ", "), "\n")

# --- Form the 4 combinations ---
selected_configs <- expand.grid(Method = best_2_models, DataVersion = best_2_dv,
                                 stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(Model = paste(Method, DataVersion))

cat("\n=== Selected 4 Configurations (2 models x 2 data versions) ===\n")
cat(paste(selected_configs$Model, collapse = "\n"), "\n")

selected_names <- selected_configs$Model
```

### ROC Curves for Selected Configurations

```{r roc-selected, fig.height=6, fig.width=8}
roc_selected <- lapply(selected_names, function(nm) {
  roc(val_labels, all_probs[[nm]],
      levels = c("neutral or dissatisfied", "satisfied"),
      direction = "<", quiet = TRUE)
})
names(roc_selected) <- selected_names

colors_sel <- c("#E74C3C", "#3498DB", "#2ECC71", "#9B59B6")

par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 3, 1), family = "sans")
plot(roc_selected[[1]], col = colors_sel[1], lwd = 2.5,
     main = "ROC Curves \u2014 Selected Model Configurations (Validation Set)",
     legacy.axes = TRUE, cex.main = 1.1, font.main = 2,
     xlab = "False Positive Rate (1 \u2212 Specificity)",
     ylab = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)")
for (i in 2:length(roc_selected)) {
  plot(roc_selected[[i]], col = colors_sel[i], lwd = 2.5,
       add = TRUE, legacy.axes = TRUE)
}
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "grey60")
legend("bottomright",
       paste0(selected_names, "  (AUC = ", round(sapply(roc_selected, auc), 4), ")"),
       col = colors_sel, lwd = 2.5, cex = 0.8, bty = "n")
```

### Confusion Matrices for Selected Configurations

```{r cm-selected, fig.height=6, fig.width=12}
cm_plots <- list()
for (i in seq_along(selected_names)) {
  nm <- selected_names[i]
  pred_class <- factor(ifelse(all_probs[[nm]] >= 0.5,
                              "satisfied", "neutral or dissatisfied"),
                       levels = levels(val_labels))
  cm <- table(Predicted = pred_class, Actual = val_labels)
  cm_df <- as.data.frame(cm)

  cm_plots[[i]] <- ggplot(cm_df, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted, fill = Freq)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1.5) +
    geom_text(aes(label = format(Freq, big.mark = ",")), size = 5, fontface = "bold") +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F7F7F7", high = "#3498DB") +
    labs(title = nm, x = "Actual Class", y = "Predicted Class") +
    theme_report(base_size = 11) +
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1),
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12))
}

grid.arrange(grobs = cm_plots, ncol = 2)
```

### Metrics Summary for Selected Configurations

```{r metrics-selected}
metrics_df %>%
  filter(Model %in% selected_names) %>%
  dplyr::select(Model, Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1, AUC) %>%
  arrange(desc(AUC)) %>%
  kable(row.names = FALSE,
        caption = "Selected Configurations — Validation Set Metrics") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
```

## Cross-Validation on 90% Data (Selected Configurations)

The single train-validation split above gave us an initial ranking, but a single split is subject to high variability — the estimated test error can fluctuate substantially depending on which observations happen to fall in the validation set. To obtain a more reliable and stable estimate of generalisation error, we perform **10-fold cross-validation** on the combined training + validation data (90% of the total) for the 4 selected model-data version configurations. The held-out 10% test set remains completely untouched until final evaluation, preserving its integrity as an unbiased estimate of true out-of-sample performance.

For KNN, which has a tuneable hyperparameter (the number of neighbours $k$), we additionally search over $k \in \{5, 10, 15, 20\}$ within the cross-validation loop.

**Pipeline approach.** A critical requirement is that *all preprocessing* must be fitted on the training folds and applied to the held-out validation fold within each iteration as discussed earlier. We use the `recipes` package from `tidymodels` to define reusable preprocessing pipelines that enforce this discipline: each recipe is `prep()`-ed on the training folds and `bake()`-d onto the validation fold, guaranteeing that no information flows in the wrong direction.

```{r cv-setup}
# install.packages("recipes")  # uncomment on first run
library(recipes)

# Combine train (80%) + validation (10%) = 90% for cross-validation
cv_data <- bind_rows(train_with_na, val_raw)
cat("CV data size (90% of total):", nrow(cv_data), "observations\n")
cat("Held-out test size (10% of total):", nrow(test_holdout), "observations\n")

set.seed(42)
n_folds <- 10
fold_ids <- createFolds(cv_data$satisfaction, k = n_folds, returnTrain = FALSE)

# Variable groups (same as before)
cont_vars <- c("age", "flight_distance",
               "departure_delay_in_minutes", "arrival_delay_in_minutes")

rating_vars <- c("inflight_wifi_service", "departure_arrival_time_convenient",
                 "ease_of_online_booking", "gate_location", "food_and_drink",
                 "online_boarding", "seat_comfort", "inflight_entertainment",
                 "on_board_service", "leg_room_service", "baggage_handling",
                 "checkin_service", "inflight_service", "cleanliness")

cat_vars <- c("gender", "customer_type", "type_of_travel", "class")

# BIC-selected variable names (from the forward BIC step earlier)
bic_predictors <- all.vars(bic_formula)[-1]  # drop the response
cat("BIC predictors:", paste(bic_predictors, collapse = ", "), "\n")
```

### Building the Preprocessing Recipes

We define three recipes — one per data version — that encode the full preprocessing pipeline:

1.  **Imputation**: predict missing `arrival_delay_in_minutes` from `departure_delay_in_minutes` using `step_impute_linear()`.
2.  **Scaling**: z-score standardisation for continuous variables (`step_normalize()`), min-max scaling for ordinal ratings (`step_range()`).
3.  **Dummy encoding**: create indicator variables for the categorical predictors (`step_dummy()`).
4.  **Variable selection** (BIC version only): keep only the BIC-selected predictors (`step_select()`).

```{r define-recipes}
# --- Base recipe (shared preprocessing steps) ---
base_recipe <- function(data) {
  recipe(satisfaction ~ ., data = data) %>%
    step_impute_linear(arrival_delay_in_minutes,
                       impute_with = imp_vars(departure_delay_in_minutes)) %>%
    step_normalize(all_of(cont_vars)) %>%
    step_range(all_of(rating_vars), min = 0, max = 1) %>%
    step_dummy(all_of(cat_vars))
}

# --- Full recipe ---
make_recipe_full <- function(data) {
  base_recipe(data)
}

# --- BIC recipe: select only BIC predictors before dummy encoding ---
make_recipe_bic <- function(data) {
  recipe(satisfaction ~ ., data = data) %>%
    step_impute_linear(arrival_delay_in_minutes,
                       impute_with = imp_vars(departure_delay_in_minutes)) %>%
    step_rm(-satisfaction, -all_of(bic_predictors)) %>%
    step_normalize(all_of(intersect(cont_vars, bic_predictors))) %>%
    step_range(all_of(intersect(rating_vars, bic_predictors)), min = 0, max = 1) %>%
    step_dummy(all_of(intersect(cat_vars, bic_predictors)))
}

#
# --- PCA recipe: full preprocessing then PCA (CV-optimal number of components) ---
#make_recipe_pca <- function(data) {
#  base_recipe(data) %>%
#    step_pca(all_predictors(), num_comp = n_pc_optimal)
#}
#
```

### Cross-Validation Loop

```{r}
selected_configs$Method[selected_configs$Method=="KNN5"] = "KNN"
selected_configs$Model[selected_configs$Model %in% c("KNN5 BIC", "KNN5 Full")] = c("KNN BIC","KNN Full")
selected_configs
```

```{r cv-loop, cache=TRUE}
# KNN k values to try
knn_k_values <- c(5,10,15)

# Helper: get AUC
get_auc <- function(probs, labels) {
  as.numeric(auc(roc(labels, probs,
                     levels = c("neutral or dissatisfied", "satisfied"),
                     direction = "<", quiet = TRUE)))
}

# Helper: get F1 score from probabilities
get_f1 <- function(probs, labels, threshold = 0.5) {
  pred_class <- factor(ifelse(probs >= threshold, "satisfied", "neutral or dissatisfied"),
                       levels = levels(labels))
  cm <- confusionMatrix(pred_class, labels, positive = "satisfied")
  prec <- cm$byClass["Precision"]
  rec  <- cm$byClass["Recall"]
  ifelse(prec + rec > 0, 2 * prec * rec / (prec + rec), 0)
}

# Storage: AUC and F1 per fold
cv_results <- list()
cv_f1_results <- list()

for (cfg_i in seq_len(nrow(selected_configs))) {
  method <- selected_configs$Method[cfg_i]
  dv     <- selected_configs$DataVersion[cfg_i]
  cfg_name <- selected_configs$Model[cfg_i]

  cat("\n=== Configuration:", cfg_name, "===\n")

  # Choose recipe constructor
  make_recipe <- switch(dv,
    "Full" = make_recipe_full,
    "BIC"  = make_recipe_bic,
    "PCA"  = make_recipe_pca
  )

  if (method == "KNN") {
    # KNN: store results for each k value
    for (k_val in knn_k_values) {
      result_name <- paste0(cfg_name, " (k=", k_val, ")")
      cv_results[[result_name]] <- numeric(n_folds)
      cv_f1_results[[result_name]] <- numeric(n_folds)
    }
  } else {
    cv_results[[cfg_name]] <- numeric(n_folds)
    cv_f1_results[[cfg_name]] <- numeric(n_folds)
  }

  for (f in seq_len(n_folds)) {
    idx_val <- fold_ids[[f]]
    tr_raw  <- cv_data[-idx_val, ]
    va_raw  <- cv_data[idx_val, ]

    # Prep recipe on training fold, bake both
    rec <- make_recipe(tr_raw) %>% prep(training = tr_raw)
    tr_baked <- bake(rec, new_data = NULL)  # NULL = return training data
    va_baked <- bake(rec, new_data = va_raw)

    va_lab <- va_baked$satisfaction

    tryCatch({
      if (method == "GLM") {
        fit <- glm(satisfaction ~ ., data = tr_baked, family = binomial)
        probs <- predict(fit, va_baked, type = "response")
        cv_results[[cfg_name]][f] <- get_auc(probs, va_lab)
        cv_f1_results[[cfg_name]][f] <- get_f1(probs, va_lab)

      } else if (method == "LDA") {
        fit <- lda(satisfaction ~ ., data = tr_baked)
        probs <- predict(fit, va_baked)$posterior[, "satisfied"]
        cv_results[[cfg_name]][f] <- get_auc(probs, va_lab)
        cv_f1_results[[cfg_name]][f] <- get_f1(probs, va_lab)

      } else if (method == "QDA") {
        fit <- qda(satisfaction ~ ., data = tr_baked)
        probs <- predict(fit, va_baked)$posterior[, "satisfied"]
        cv_results[[cfg_name]][f] <- get_auc(probs, va_lab)
        cv_f1_results[[cfg_name]][f] <- get_f1(probs, va_lab)

      } else if (method == "NB") {
        fit <- naiveBayes(satisfaction ~ ., data = tr_baked)
        probs <- predict(fit, va_baked, type = "raw")[, "satisfied"]
        cv_results[[cfg_name]][f] <- get_auc(probs, va_lab)
        cv_f1_results[[cfg_name]][f] <- get_f1(probs, va_lab)

      } else if (method == "KNN") {
        # Prepare numeric matrices for knn()
        tr_x <- tr_baked %>% dplyr::select(-satisfaction) %>% as.data.frame()
        va_x <- va_baked %>% dplyr::select(-satisfaction) %>% as.data.frame()
        tr_y <- tr_baked$satisfaction

        for (k_val in knn_k_values) {
          result_name <- paste0(cfg_name, " (k=", k_val, ")")
          knn_pred <- knn(tr_x, va_x, cl = tr_y, k = k_val, prob = TRUE)
          knn_pr   <- attr(knn_pred, "prob")
          probs    <- ifelse(knn_pred == "satisfied", knn_pr, 1 - knn_pr)
          cv_results[[result_name]][f] <- get_auc(probs, va_lab)
          cv_f1_results[[result_name]][f] <- get_f1(probs, va_lab)
        }
      }
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat("  Error in fold", f, ":", conditionMessage(e), "\n")
    })
  }
  cat("  Done.\n")
}

cat("\n10-fold cross-validation complete.\n")
```

### Cross-Validation AUC Results

```{r cv-results-table}
cv_summary <- data.frame(
  Configuration = names(cv_results),
  Mean_AUC      = round(sapply(cv_results, mean, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
  SD_AUC        = round(sapply(cv_results, sd, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
  Mean_F1       = round(sapply(cv_f1_results, mean, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
  SD_F1         = round(sapply(cv_f1_results, sd, na.rm = TRUE), 4)
) %>%
  arrange(desc(Mean_AUC))

cv_summary %>%
  kable(row.names = FALSE,
        col.names = c("Configuration", "Mean AUC", "SD AUC", "Mean F1", "SD F1"),
        caption = "10-Fold Cross-Validation Results (90% Data: Train + Validation)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
```

```{r cv-boxplot, fig.height=6, fig.width=10}
cv_long <- stack(cv_results) %>%
  rename(AUC = values, Configuration = ind)

cv_long$Configuration <- factor(cv_long$Configuration,
                                 levels = rev(cv_summary$Configuration))

ggplot(cv_long, aes(x = Configuration, y = AUC, fill = Configuration)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE, width = 0.6) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(title = "10-Fold Cross-Validation: AUC Distribution by Configuration",
       subtitle = "KNN configurations include multiple values of k; box shows inter-fold variability",
       x = NULL, y = "AUC") +
  theme_report(base_size = 12)
```

### Best Configuration

```{r best-cv-config}
best_cv <- cv_summary$Configuration[1]
best_cv_auc <- cv_summary$Mean_AUC[1]
best_cv_sd  <- cv_summary$SD_AUC[1]

cat("=== Best Configuration by Mean 10-Fold CV AUC ===\n")
cat("Configuration:", best_cv, "\n")
cat("Mean AUC:", best_cv_auc, "(±", best_cv_sd, ")\n\n")

# If best is a KNN variant, extract the best k
if (grepl("KNN", best_cv)) {
  best_k <- as.numeric(gsub(".*k=(\\d+).*", "\\1", best_cv))
  cat("Best k for KNN:", best_k, "\n")
}

cat("\nFull ranking:\n")
for (i in seq_len(nrow(cv_summary))) {
  cat(sprintf("  %d. %s — Mean AUC: %.4f (± %.4f)\n",
              i, cv_summary$Configuration[i],
              cv_summary$Mean_AUC[i], cv_summary$SD_AUC[i]))
}
```

## Logistic Regression with L1 and L2 Regularisation

```{r}
#install.packages("tidymodels")
library(tidymodels)
library(glmnet)
library(pROC)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tibble)

#--------------------------------------------------
# AUC helper
#--------------------------------------------------
get_auc <- function(probs, labels) {
  as.numeric(auc(
    roc(labels, probs,
        levels = c("neutral or dissatisfied", "satisfied"),
        direction = "<",
        quiet = TRUE)
  ))
}

# glmnet needs 0/1 outcome for binomial
to_binary01 <- function(y) {
  ifelse(y == "satisfied", 1, 0)
}

#--------------------------------------------------
# CV over a lambda grid for penalized logistic regression
# alpha = 0 -> ridge
# alpha = 1 -> lasso
#--------------------------------------------------
cv_glmnet_grid <- function(data,
                           fold_ids,
                           make_recipe,
                           lambda_grid,
                           alpha_value,
                           model_name = "ridge") {

  n_folds <- length(fold_ids)

  # rows = lambdas, cols = folds
  auc_mat <- matrix(NA_real_,
                    nrow = length(lambda_grid),
                    ncol = n_folds,
                    dimnames = list(paste0("lambda_", seq_along(lambda_grid)),
                                    paste0("fold_", seq_len(n_folds))))

  for (f in seq_len(n_folds)) {
    cat("\n=== ", model_name, " | fold ", f, "/", n_folds, " ===\n", sep = "")

    idx_val <- fold_ids[[f]]
    tr_raw  <- data[-idx_val, ]
    va_raw  <- data[idx_val, ]

    # Prep recipe on training fold only
    rec <- make_recipe(tr_raw) %>% prep(training = tr_raw)

    tr_baked <- bake(rec, new_data = NULL)
    va_baked <- bake(rec, new_data = va_raw)

    x_train <- tr_baked %>%
      dplyr::select(-satisfaction) %>%
      as.matrix()

    x_valid <- va_baked %>%
      dplyr::select(-satisfaction) %>%
      as.matrix()

    y_train <- to_binary01(tr_baked$satisfaction)
    y_valid <- va_baked$satisfaction

    for (i in seq_along(lambda_grid)) {
      lam <- lambda_grid[i]

      fit <- glmnet(
        x = x_train,
        y = y_train,
        family = "binomial",
        alpha = alpha_value,
        lambda = lam,
        standardize = FALSE   # already scaled in recipe
      )

      probs <- as.numeric(
        predict(fit, newx = x_valid, type = "response")
      )

      auc_mat[i, f] <- get_auc(probs, y_valid)
    }
  }

  summary_df <- tibble(
    Model = model_name,
    lambda = lambda_grid,
    log_lambda = log(lambda_grid),
    mean_auc = rowMeans(auc_mat, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_auc = apply(auc_mat, 1, sd, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

  list(
    auc_mat = auc_mat,
    summary = summary_df
  )
}
```

```{r}
lambda_grid <- 10^seq(-5, 2, length.out = 20)
ridge_cv <- cv_glmnet_grid(
  data = cv_data,
  fold_ids = fold_ids,
  make_recipe = make_recipe_full,
  lambda_grid = lambda_grid,
  alpha_value = 0,
  model_name = "Ridge Full"
)

lasso_cv <- cv_glmnet_grid(
  data = cv_data,
  fold_ids = fold_ids,
  make_recipe = make_recipe_full,
  lambda_grid = lambda_grid,
  alpha_value = 1,
  model_name = "Lasso Full"
)
ridge_cv$summary
lasso_cv$summary
```

```{r ridge-cv-plot, fig.height=5, fig.width=8}
ggplot(ridge_cv$summary, aes(x = lambda, y = mean_auc)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_auc - sd_auc, ymax = mean_auc + sd_auc),
              alpha = 0.15, fill = "#3498DB") +
  geom_line(color = "#3498DB", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#3498DB", size = 2.5) +
  geom_point(data = ridge_cv$summary %>% slice_max(mean_auc, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE),
             shape = 21, size = 5, stroke = 1.2, color = "#3498DB", fill = "white") +
  geom_text(data = ridge_cv$summary %>% slice_max(mean_auc, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE),
            aes(label = sprintf("%.4f", mean_auc)),
            vjust = -1.4, size = 4, fontface = "bold", color = "#2C3E50") +
  scale_x_log10(labels = scales::label_number()) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0.80, 0.95)) +
  labs(title = "Ridge (L2) Logistic Regression: 10-Fold CV AUC",
       subtitle = expression("Shaded band = \u00b1 1 SD across folds; circled point = optimal " * lambda),
       x = expression(Regularisation~Parameter~lambda ~ "(log scale)"),
       y = "Mean Cross-Validated AUC") +
  theme_report()
```

```{r lasso-cv-plot, fig.height=5, fig.width=8}
ggplot(lasso_cv$summary, aes(x = lambda, y = mean_auc)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_auc - sd_auc, ymax = mean_auc + sd_auc),
              alpha = 0.15, fill = "#E74C3C") +
  geom_line(color = "#E74C3C", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#E74C3C", size = 2.5) +
  geom_point(data = lasso_cv$summary %>% slice_max(mean_auc, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE),
             shape = 21, size = 5, stroke = 1.2, color = "#E74C3C", fill = "white") +
  geom_text(data = lasso_cv$summary %>% slice_max(mean_auc, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE),
            aes(label = sprintf("%.4f", mean_auc)),
            vjust = -1.4, size = 4, fontface = "bold", color = "#2C3E50") +
  scale_x_log10(labels = scales::label_number()) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0.45, 0.95)) +
  labs(title = "Lasso (L1) Logistic Regression: 10-Fold CV AUC",
       subtitle = expression("Shaded band = \u00b1 1 SD across folds; circled point = optimal " * lambda),
       x = expression(Regularisation~Parameter~lambda ~ "(log scale)"),
       y = "Mean Cross-Validated AUC") +
  theme_report()
```

```{r lasso-cv-plot, fig.height=5, fig.width=8}
ggplot(lasso_cv$summary, aes(x = lambda, y = mean_auc)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_auc - sd_auc, ymax = mean_auc + sd_auc),
              alpha = 0.15, fill = "#E74C3C") +
  geom_line(color = "#E74C3C", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#E74C3C", size = 2.5) +
  geom_point(data = lasso_cv$summary %>% slice_max(mean_auc, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE),
             shape = 21, size = 5, stroke = 1.2, color = "#E74C3C", fill = "white") +
  geom_text(data = lasso_cv$summary %>% slice_max(mean_auc, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE),
            aes(label = sprintf("%.4f", mean_auc)),
            vjust = -1.4, size = 4, fontface = "bold", color = "#2C3E50") +
  scale_x_log10(labels = scales::label_number()) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0.85, 0.95)) +
  labs(title = "Lasso (L1) Logistic Regression: 10-Fold CV AUC",
       subtitle = expression("Shaded band = \u00b1 1 SD across folds; circled point = optimal " * lambda),
       x = expression(Regularisation~Parameter~lambda ~ "(log scale)"),
       y = "Mean Cross-Validated AUC") +
  theme_report()
```

```{r lasso-cv-plot, fig.height=5, fig.width=8}
lasso_cv$summary %>% slice_max(mean_auc, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)
ridge_cv$summary %>% slice_max(mean_auc, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)
```

### Why Penalised Methods?

Ridge and Lasso regularisation add a penalty term to the likelihood, shrinking coefficient estimates toward zero and trading a small increase in bias for a possible reduction in variance. Ridge (L2) shrinks all coefficients continuously, while Lasso (L1) can shrink some coefficients exactly to zero, thereby performing implicit variable selection. We consider both methods here because the model contains 23 predictors, including several service-rating variables that may carry overlapping information, so regularisation may improve stability and offer a useful comparison with the subset-selection approaches considered earlier.

### Results and Interpretation

Both Ridge and Lasso achieve their highest cross-validated AUC at extremely small values of $\lambda$, indicating that only negligible regularisation is preferred. Specifically, the optimal Ridge value is $\lambda = 0.0001274$ with mean CV AUC $= 0.9267$, while the optimal Lasso value is $\lambda = 0.0002976$ with mean CV AUC $= 0.9267$. These values are so close to zero that both penalised models are effectively equivalent to the unpenalised full logistic regression model.

As $\lambda$ increases, performance becomes worse for both methods. Ridge declines gradually, reflecting the fact that L2 regularisation continuously shrinks all coefficients without eliminating them. Lasso shows a sharper drop for moderate and large $\lambda$, and eventually approaches AUC $= 0.5$, indicating that excessive L1 penalisation shrinks all coefficients to zero and the model loses predictive ability.

Overall, neither penalised method improves meaningfully over the unpenalised logistic model.

## Generalized Additive Models (GAMs)

Logistic regression assumes that each predictor exerts a strictly linear effect on the log-odds of the response. GAMs relax this assumption by replacing each linear term $\beta_j x_j$ with a flexible smooth function $f_j(x_j)$, while preserving the additive structure that makes the model interpretable:

$$\log\!\left(\frac{p}{1-p}\right) = \beta_0 + f_1(x_1) + f_2(x_2) + \cdots + f_p(x_p)$$

This allows each predictor to exert a potentially nonlinear effect on the log-odds of satisfaction. We focus on logistic GAMs fit directly with `mgcv::gam()` and compare nested specifications via AIC, ANOVA, ROC curves, and cross-validation.

```{r gam-libraries, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(mgcv)
```

### Data Preparation for GAMs

The GAM analysis uses the **BIC-reduced predictor set**, which drops `flight_distance`, `gate_location`, `food_and_drink`, and `gender` from the full model. The three BIC continuous predictors are `age`, `departure_delay_in_minutes`, and `arrival_delay_in_minutes`.

```{r}
bic_predictors
```

```{r gam-data-prep, warning=FALSE}
# BIC continuous predictors: age, departure_delay, arrival_delay
# (flight_distance dropped by BIC)
bic_cont_gam   <- intersect(cont_vars,   bic_predictors)
bic_rating_gam <- intersect(rating_vars, bic_predictors)
bic_cat_gam    <- intersect(cat_vars,    bic_predictors)

cat("BIC continuous predictors for GAMs:", paste(bic_cont_gam, collapse = ", "), "\n")

# Build GAM training data from 'train' (already imputed, satisfaction as factor)
train_gam <- train %>%
  dplyr::select(satisfaction, all_of(bic_predictors))

# Z-score continuous (using training data parameters)
gam_cont_means <- colMeans(train_gam[, bic_cont_gam])
gam_cont_sds   <- apply(train_gam[, bic_cont_gam], 2, sd)
train_gam[, bic_cont_gam] <- scale(train_gam[, bic_cont_gam],
                                    center = gam_cont_means,
                                    scale  = gam_cont_sds)

# Min-max rating variables
gam_rating_mins   <- apply(train_gam[, bic_rating_gam], 2, min)
gam_rating_ranges <- apply(train_gam[, bic_rating_gam], 2, max) - gam_rating_mins
for (v in bic_rating_gam) {
  train_gam[[v]] <- (train_gam[[v]] - gam_rating_mins[v]) / gam_rating_ranges[v]
}

cat("train_gam dimensions:", dim(train_gam), "\n")
cat("Remaining NAs:", sum(is.na(train_gam)), "\n")
```

### Fitting a Logistic GAM (Section 7.7.2)

We now fit the logistic GAMs. Ratings and categorical predictors remain linear, while the three continuous predictors are progressively allowed to enter as smooth terms across the nested specifications.

```{r gam-full-fit, warning=FALSE}
gam_fit_full <- mgcv::gam(
  satisfaction ~ 
    s(age) +
    s(departure_delay_in_minutes) +
    s(arrival_delay_in_minutes) +
    online_boarding + seat_comfort + inflight_entertainment + on_board_service +
    leg_room_service + baggage_handling + checkin_service + inflight_service + cleanliness +
    ease_of_online_booking + inflight_wifi_service + departure_arrival_time_convenient +
    customer_type + type_of_travel + class,
  data = train_gam,
  family = binomial,
  method = "REML"
)
summary(gam_fit_full)
```

```{r gam-smooth-plots, fig.height=4, fig.width=12, warning=FALSE}
# Plot the estimated smooth functions with +/- 2 SE confidence bands
par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(5, 5, 3, 1), family = "sans")
plot(gam_fit_full, se = TRUE, col = "#3498DB", lwd = 2.5,
     shade = TRUE, shade.col = adjustcolor("#3498DB", alpha.f = 0.15),
     cex.lab = 1.2, cex.axis = 1.1, cex.main = 1.2)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```

These smooth plots display the estimated partial effect of each continuous predictor on the log-odds of satisfaction, holding the remaining predictors fixed. The dashed curves represent approximate $\pm 2$ standard error bands around each smooth.

For `age`, the estimated smooth is nearly flat, with only a very mild decline at higher values, suggesting little evidence of a strong nonlinear effect. For `departure_delay_in_minutes`, the fitted smooth is also essentially flat, and the confidence bands widen substantially at larger delays, indicating high uncertainty and limited evidence of a meaningful effect. For `arrival_delay_in_minutes`, the smooth shows some mild fluctuation, but the uncertainty becomes very large in the upper range, so any observed nonlinearity should be interpreted cautiously.

Overall, the GAM smooths suggest that these continuous predictors contribute only weakly and that there is no strong or stable nonlinear pattern beyond minor fluctuations.

### GAM Model Comparison

We compare four nested GAM specifications fitted on `train_gam` (BIC predictors), varying whether the three continuous predictors enter linearly or as smooth terms:

-   **M1**: All BIC predictors enter linearly (equivalent to BIC logistic regression)
-   **M2**: `age` gets a smooth spline; `departure_delay` and `arrival_delay` remain linear
-   **M3**: `age` and `departure_delay` get a smooth spline: `arrival_delay` remain linear
-   **M4**: `age`, `departure_delay` and `arrival_delay` get a smooth spline

The nesting M1 ⊂ M2 ⊂ M3 allows chi-squared deviance tests of whether each additional smooth term significantly improves fit.

```{r gam-lab-models, warning=FALSE}
# Ratings + categorical predictors remain linear in every specification.

# M1: all BIC predictors linear
gam_m1 <- mgcv::gam(
  satisfaction ~ 
    age +
    departure_delay_in_minutes + arrival_delay_in_minutes +
    online_boarding + seat_comfort + inflight_entertainment + on_board_service +
    leg_room_service + baggage_handling + checkin_service + inflight_service + cleanliness +
    ease_of_online_booking + inflight_wifi_service + departure_arrival_time_convenient +
    customer_type + type_of_travel + class,
  data = train_gam, family = binomial, method = "REML"
)

# M2: smooth age
gam_m2 <- mgcv::gam(
  satisfaction ~ 
    s(age) +
    departure_delay_in_minutes + arrival_delay_in_minutes +
    online_boarding + seat_comfort + inflight_entertainment + on_board_service +
    leg_room_service + baggage_handling + checkin_service + inflight_service + cleanliness +
    ease_of_online_booking + inflight_wifi_service + departure_arrival_time_convenient +
    customer_type + type_of_travel + class,
  data = train_gam, family = binomial, method = "REML"
)

# M3: smooth age and departure delay
gam_m3 <- mgcv::gam(
  satisfaction ~ 
    s(age) + s(departure_delay_in_minutes) + arrival_delay_in_minutes +
    online_boarding + seat_comfort + inflight_entertainment + on_board_service +
    leg_room_service + baggage_handling + checkin_service + inflight_service + cleanliness +
    ease_of_online_booking + inflight_wifi_service + departure_arrival_time_convenient +
    customer_type + type_of_travel + class,
  data = train_gam, family = binomial, method = "REML"
)

# M4: smooth age, departure delay, and arrival delay
gam_m4 <- mgcv::gam(
  satisfaction ~ 
    s(age) + s(departure_delay_in_minutes) + s(arrival_delay_in_minutes) +
    online_boarding + seat_comfort + inflight_entertainment + on_board_service +
    leg_room_service + baggage_handling + checkin_service + inflight_service + cleanliness +
    ease_of_online_booking + inflight_wifi_service + departure_arrival_time_convenient +
    customer_type + type_of_travel + class,
  data = train_gam, family = binomial, method = "REML"
)

cat("=== AIC Comparison (BIC data) ===\n")
cat(sprintf("M1 (All linear)                         : AIC = %.2f\n", AIC(gam_m1)))
cat(sprintf("M2 (Smooth age)                        : AIC = %.2f\n", AIC(gam_m2)))
cat(sprintf("M3 (Smooth age + departure delay)      : AIC = %.2f\n", AIC(gam_m3)))
cat(sprintf("M4 (Smooth age + departure + arrival)  : AIC = %.2f\n", AIC(gam_m4)))  
```

```{r gam-anova, warning=FALSE}
# Formal chi-squared deviance test across the nested GAM sequence
anova(gam_m1, gam_m2,gam_m3,gam_m4, test = "Chisq")
```

```{r gam-training-auc, warning=FALSE}
lab_train_gam <- train_gam$satisfaction

prob_gam_m1 <- predict(gam_m1, type = "response")
prob_gam_m2 <- predict(gam_m2, type = "response")
prob_gam_m3 <- predict(gam_m3, type = "response")
prob_gam_m4 <- predict(gam_m4, type = "response")

cat("\n=== Training AUC Comparison ===\n")
cat(sprintf("M1 (All linear)                          : %.4f\n",
            get_auc(prob_gam_m1,   lab_train_gam)))
cat(sprintf("M2 (Smooth age)                          : %.4f\n",
            get_auc(prob_gam_m2, lab_train_gam)))
cat(sprintf("M3 (Smooth age + departure delay)        : %.4f\n",
            get_auc(prob_gam_m3, lab_train_gam)))
cat(sprintf("M4 (Smooth age + departure + arrival)    : %.4f\n",
            get_auc(prob_gam_m4, lab_train_gam)))
```

```{r gam-roc-comparison, fig.height=5, fig.width=8, warning=FALSE}
roc_gam_m1 <- roc(lab_train_gam, prob_gam_m1,
                  levels = c("neutral or dissatisfied","satisfied"),
                  direction = "<", quiet = TRUE)
roc_gam_m2 <- roc(lab_train_gam, prob_gam_m2,
                  levels = c("neutral or dissatisfied","satisfied"),
                  direction = "<", quiet = TRUE)
roc_gam_m3 <- roc(lab_train_gam, prob_gam_m3,
                  levels = c("neutral or dissatisfied","satisfied"),
                  direction = "<", quiet = TRUE)
roc_gam_m4 <- roc(lab_train_gam, prob_gam_m4,
                  levels = c("neutral or dissatisfied","satisfied"),
                  direction = "<", quiet = TRUE)

par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 3, 1), family = "sans")
plot(roc_gam_m1, col = "#E74C3C", lwd = 2.5,
     main = "ROC Curves \u2014 Nested GAM Specifications (Training Set)",
     legacy.axes = TRUE, cex.main = 1.1, font.main = 2,
     xlab = "False Positive Rate (1 \u2212 Specificity)",
     ylab = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)")
plot(roc_gam_m2, col = "#E67E22", lwd = 2.5, add = TRUE, legacy.axes = TRUE)
plot(roc_gam_m3, col = "#2ECC71", lwd = 2.5, add = TRUE, legacy.axes = TRUE)
plot(roc_gam_m4, col = "#3498DB", lwd = 2.5, add = TRUE, legacy.axes = TRUE)
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "grey60")
legend("bottomright",
       legend = c(
         sprintf("M1: All Linear (AUC = %.4f)", auc(roc_gam_m1)),
         sprintf("M2: Smooth age (AUC = %.4f)", auc(roc_gam_m2)),
         sprintf("M3: + Smooth dep. delay (AUC = %.4f)", auc(roc_gam_m3)),
         sprintf("M4: + Smooth arr. delay (AUC = %.4f)", auc(roc_gam_m4))
       ),
       col = c("#E74C3C", "#E67E22", "#2ECC71", "#3498DB"),
       lwd = 2.5, cex = 0.8, bty = "n")
```

### Cross-Validation for GAM Models

Training AUC is optimistic. We apply 10-fold CV using the same pipeline discipline as throughout: all preprocessing is fitted on each training fold and applied to the held-out fold. We define a dedicated recipe for GAMs that uses only the BIC predictors with standard scaling.

```{r gam-recipe}
# BIC recipe for GAMs
make_recipe_bic_gam <- function(data) {
  recipe(satisfaction ~ ., data = data) %>%
    # 1. Impute arrival_delay from departure_delay
    step_impute_linear(arrival_delay_in_minutes,
                       impute_with = imp_vars(departure_delay_in_minutes)) %>%
    # 2. Drop non-BIC predictors
    step_rm(-satisfaction, -all_of(bic_predictors)) %>%
    # 3. Z-score continuous, min-max ratings, dummy encode categoricals
    step_normalize(all_of(intersect(cont_vars,   bic_predictors))) %>%
    step_range(all_of(intersect(rating_vars, bic_predictors)), min = 0, max = 1) %>%
    step_dummy(all_of(intersect(cat_vars,    bic_predictors)))
}
```

```{r gam-cv, cache=TRUE, warning=FALSE}
gam_m1_aucs <- numeric(n_folds)
gam_m2_aucs <- numeric(n_folds)
gam_m3_aucs <- numeric(n_folds)
gam_m4_aucs <- numeric(n_folds)

for (f in seq_len(n_folds)) {
  cat(sprintf("\rGAM CV: fold %d / %d   ", f, n_folds))

  idx_val <- fold_ids[[f]]
  tr_raw  <- cv_data[-idx_val, ]
  va_raw  <- cv_data[idx_val, ]

  # BIC recipe: fitted on training fold, applied to both
  rec    <- make_recipe_bic_gam(tr_raw) %>% prep(training = tr_raw)
  tr_bak <- bake(rec, new_data = NULL)
  va_bak <- bake(rec, new_data = va_raw)
  va_lab <- va_bak$satisfaction

  # Identify variable groups in the baked output
  baked_cols   <- colnames(tr_bak)
  cont_baked   <- intersect(cont_vars, baked_cols)   # age, dep_delay, arr_delay
  rating_baked <- intersect(rating_vars, baked_cols)
  dummy_baked  <- setdiff(baked_cols, c("satisfaction", cont_baked, rating_baked))

  # Variable names after baking
  age_col       <- intersect("age", cont_baked)
  dep_delay_col <- intersect("departure_delay_in_minutes", cont_baked)
  arr_delay_col <- intersect("arrival_delay_in_minutes", cont_baked)

  # Common linear terms
  lin_common <- c(rating_baked, dummy_baked)

  # RHS for each model
  rhs_m1 <- paste(c(age_col, dep_delay_col, arr_delay_col, lin_common), collapse = " + ")
  rhs_m2 <- paste(c("s(age)", dep_delay_col, arr_delay_col, lin_common), collapse = " + ")
  rhs_m3 <- paste(c("s(age)", "s(departure_delay_in_minutes)", arr_delay_col, lin_common), collapse = " + ")
  rhs_m4 <- paste(c("s(age)", "s(departure_delay_in_minutes)", "s(arrival_delay_in_minutes)", lin_common), collapse = " + ")

  tryCatch({
    # M1: all BIC predictors linear
    fit_m1 <- mgcv::gam(
      as.formula(paste("satisfaction ~", rhs_m1)),
      data = tr_bak, family = binomial, method = "REML"
    )
    gam_m1_aucs[f] <- get_auc(predict(fit_m1, va_bak, type = "response"), va_lab)

    # M2: smooth age
    fit_m2 <- mgcv::gam(
      as.formula(paste("satisfaction ~", rhs_m2)),
      data = tr_bak, family = binomial, method = "REML"
    )
    gam_m2_aucs[f] <- get_auc(predict(fit_m2, va_bak, type = "response"), va_lab)

    # M3: smooth age + smooth departure delay
    fit_m3 <- mgcv::gam(
      as.formula(paste("satisfaction ~", rhs_m3)),
      data = tr_bak, family = binomial, method = "REML"
    )
    gam_m3_aucs[f] <- get_auc(predict(fit_m3, va_bak, type = "response"), va_lab)

    # M4: smooth age + smooth departure delay + smooth arrival delay
    fit_m4 <- mgcv::gam(
      as.formula(paste("satisfaction ~", rhs_m4)),
      data = tr_bak, family = binomial, method = "REML"
    )
    gam_m4_aucs[f] <- get_auc(predict(fit_m4, va_bak, type = "response"), va_lab)

  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("\n  Error in fold", f, ":", conditionMessage(e), "\n")
  })
}
cat("\nGAM cross-validation complete.\n")
```

```{r gam-cv-results}
gam_cv_df <- data.frame(
  Model = c("M1: BIC linear (logistic)",
            "M2: Smooth age",
            "M3: Smooth age + departure delay",
            "M4: Smooth age + departure + arrival delay"),
  
  Mean_AUC = round(c(mean(gam_m1_aucs, na.rm = TRUE),
                     mean(gam_m2_aucs, na.rm = TRUE),
                     mean(gam_m3_aucs, na.rm = TRUE),
                     mean(gam_m4_aucs, na.rm = TRUE)), 4),
  
  SD_AUC = round(c(sd(gam_m1_aucs, na.rm = TRUE),
                   sd(gam_m2_aucs, na.rm = TRUE),
                   sd(gam_m3_aucs, na.rm = TRUE),
                   sd(gam_m4_aucs, na.rm = TRUE)), 4)
) %>%
  arrange(desc(Mean_AUC))

gam_cv_df %>%
  kable(row.names = FALSE,
        col.names = c("Model", "Mean CV AUC", "SD CV AUC"),
        caption = "10-fold CV AUC: GAM Specifications (BIC data, pipeline)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
```

```{r gam-cv-boxplot, fig.height=4, fig.width=9, warning=FALSE}
gam_cv_long <- data.frame(
  AUC = c(gam_m1_aucs, gam_m2_aucs, gam_m3_aucs, gam_m4_aucs),
  Model = rep(
    c("M1: All Linear",
      "M2: Smooth age",
      "M3: Smooth age + dep delay",
      "M4: Smooth age + dep + arr delay"),
    each = n_folds
  )
)

gam_cv_long$Model <- factor(
  gam_cv_long$Model,
  levels = c("M1: All Linear",
             "M2: Smooth age",
             "M3: Smooth age + dep delay",
             "M4: Smooth age + dep + arr delay")
)

ggplot(gam_cv_long, aes(x = Model, y = AUC, fill = Model)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE, width = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#E74C3C", "#E67E22", "#2ECC71", "#3498DB")) +
  labs(
    title = "10-Fold Cross-Validated AUC: GAM Specifications",
    subtitle = "All preprocessing fitted within each fold; boxes display inter-fold variability",
    x = NULL, y = "AUC"
  ) +
  theme_report() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 15, hjust = 1))
```

### GAM Results and Interpretation

The ANOVA chi-squared comparisons indicate that introducing smooth terms for the continuous predictors (`age`, `departure_delay_in_minutes`, and `arrival_delay_in_minutes`) leads to a statistically significant improvement in model fit. Consistent with this, the cross-validation results show that the GAM specification with smooth terms for all three variables (M4) achieves the highest CV AUC among the GAM models and outperforms the baseline logistic regression fitted on the BIC-selected data.

However, the improvement over the linear logistic model remains modest. When compared to alternative methods, the KNN models—particularly with $k = 15$ and $k = 10$—achieve higher cross-validated AUC values.

Therefore, although the GAM model provides a better fit than the standard logistic regression by capturing mild nonlinear effects, it does not match the predictive performance of the top KNN models.

## Threshold Optimisation for Top KNN Models

All classifiers evaluated so far used the default decision threshold $t = 0.50$: an observation is classified as "satisfied" if $\hat{P}(\text{satisfied} \mid X) \geq 0.50$. However the optimal threshold depends on the relative costs of false positives and false negatives and on the class prior. With our moderate class imbalance (\$\sim\$57% dissatisfied), the optimal threshold may deviate from 0.50. We therefore perform a post-hoc threshold sweep to identify the value that maximises the F1 score — a metric that balances precision and recall and is more appropriate than accuracy in the presence of class imbalance.

The analysis so far identified two KNN configurations as the top performers: **KNN (**$k = 15$) with a mean CV AUC of 0.9777 and mean CV F1 of 0.9118, and **KNN (**$k = 10$) with a mean CV AUC of 0.9757 and mean CV F1 of 0.9165. We retrain each model on the full training set and sweep across a fine threshold grid $t \in [0.01, 0.99]$ on the validation set to identify the F1-maximising threshold for each.

```{r threshold-optimisation, fig.height=5, fig.width=9}
# --- Retrain both KNN models on train set, predict on validation set ---
# Preprocess: fit BIC recipe on train_with_na, bake both train and val
rec_bic <- make_recipe_bic(train_with_na) %>% prep(training = train_with_na)
tr_baked_bic <- bake(rec_bic, new_data = NULL)
va_baked_bic <- bake(rec_bic, new_data = val_raw)

tr_x <- tr_baked_bic %>% dplyr::select(-satisfaction) %>% as.data.frame()
va_x <- va_baked_bic %>% dplyr::select(-satisfaction) %>% as.data.frame()
tr_y <- tr_baked_bic$satisfaction
va_y <- va_baked_bic$satisfaction

# KNN k=15
knn15_pred <- knn(tr_x, va_x, cl = tr_y, k = 15, prob = TRUE)
knn15_prob_raw <- attr(knn15_pred, "prob")
prob_knn15 <- ifelse(knn15_pred == "satisfied", knn15_prob_raw, 1 - knn15_prob_raw)

# KNN k=10
knn10_pred <- knn(tr_x, va_x, cl = tr_y, k = 10, prob = TRUE)
knn10_prob_raw <- attr(knn10_pred, "prob")
prob_knn10 <- ifelse(knn10_pred == "satisfied", knn10_prob_raw, 1 - knn10_prob_raw)

# --- Sweep threshold grid ---
threshold_grid <- seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01)

compute_f1_at_threshold <- function(probs, labels, threshold) {
  pred_class <- factor(ifelse(probs >= threshold, "satisfied", "neutral or dissatisfied"),
                       levels = levels(labels))
  cm <- confusionMatrix(pred_class, labels, positive = "satisfied")
  prec <- cm$byClass["Precision"]
  rec  <- cm$byClass["Recall"]
  if (is.na(prec) | is.na(rec) | (prec + rec == 0)) return(0)
  2 * prec * rec / (prec + rec)
}

f1_k15 <- sapply(threshold_grid, function(t) compute_f1_at_threshold(prob_knn15, va_y, t))
f1_k10 <- sapply(threshold_grid, function(t) compute_f1_at_threshold(prob_knn10, va_y, t))

# Build data frame for plotting
f1_df <- bind_rows(
  tibble(Threshold = threshold_grid, F1 = f1_k15, Model = "KNN (k = 15)"),
  tibble(Threshold = threshold_grid, F1 = f1_k10, Model = "KNN (k = 10)")
)

# Find optimal thresholds
best_t15 <- threshold_grid[which.max(f1_k15)]
best_f1_15 <- max(f1_k15)
best_t10 <- threshold_grid[which.max(f1_k10)]
best_f1_10 <- max(f1_k10)

best_points <- tibble(
  Threshold = c(best_t15, best_t10),
  F1 = c(best_f1_15, best_f1_10),
  Model = c("KNN (k = 15)", "KNN (k = 10)"),
  Label = c(sprintf("t* = %.2f, F1 = %.4f", best_t15, best_f1_15),
            sprintf("t* = %.2f, F1 = %.4f", best_t10, best_f1_10))
)

cat("=== Optimal Thresholds (Validation Set) ===\n")
cat(sprintf("KNN k=15: threshold = %.2f, F1 = %.4f\n", best_t15, best_f1_15))
cat(sprintf("KNN k=10: threshold = %.2f, F1 = %.4f\n", best_t10, best_f1_10))

# --- Plot ---
ggplot(f1_df, aes(x = Threshold, y = F1, color = Model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  # Coloured vertical lines at optimal thresholds (replace inline labels)
  geom_vline(xintercept = best_t15, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "#3498DB", linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = best_t10, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "#E74C3C", linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
  # Optimal points on the curves
  geom_point(data = best_points, aes(x = Threshold, y = F1),
             shape = 21, size = 5, stroke = 1.3, fill = "white") +
  # Default threshold
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dotted",
             color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.515, y = min(f1_df$F1) + 0.003,
           label = "Default\n(t = 0.5)", hjust = 0, size = 3, color = "grey50") +
  # Clean summary box bottom-right — no overlap possible
  annotate("label",
           x = 0.68, y = min(f1_df$F1) + 0.006,
           label = sprintf("KNN k=15:  t* = %.2f,  F1 = %.4f\nKNN k=10:  t* = %.2f,  F1 = %.4f",
                           best_t15, best_f1_15, best_t10, best_f1_10),
           hjust = 0, size = 3.5, color = "grey20", fill = "white",
           label.padding = ggplot2::unit(0.4, "lines"), label.size = 0.3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("KNN (k = 15)" = "#3498DB",
                                "KNN (k = 10)" = "#E74C3C")) +
  labs(
    title = "F1 Score vs. Classification Threshold \u2014 Top KNN Models",
    subtitle = "Dashed vertical lines mark the F1-maximising threshold for each model",
    x = "Classification Threshold (t)", y = "F1 Score",
    color = "Model"
  ) +
  theme_report()
```

# Final Model: KNN ($k = 15$) — Held-Out Test Set Evaluation

Having completed all model fitting and threshold tuning using only the training and validation data, we now evaluate our selected model — **KNN with** $k = 15$ on the BIC-reduced predictor set — on the **held-out test set** that has been completely untouched throughout the analysis. This is the definitive assessment of generalisation performance. The optimised classification threshold from the validation sweep is $t^* = 0.34$.

```{r final-model-test, fig.height=6, fig.width=8}
# --- Preprocess: fit BIC recipe on train_with_na, bake train and test_holdout ---
rec_final <- make_recipe_bic(train_with_na) %>% prep(training = train_with_na)
tr_baked <- bake(rec_final, new_data = NULL)
te_baked <- bake(rec_final, new_data = test_holdout)

tr_x_final <- tr_baked %>% dplyr::select(-satisfaction) %>% as.data.frame()
te_x_final <- te_baked %>% dplyr::select(-satisfaction) %>% as.data.frame()
tr_y_final <- tr_baked$satisfaction
te_y_final <- te_baked$satisfaction

# --- KNN k=15 prediction on test set ---
set.seed(42)
knn_test_pred <- knn(tr_x_final, te_x_final, cl = tr_y_final, k = 15, prob = TRUE)
knn_test_prob_raw <- attr(knn_test_pred, "prob")
prob_test_satisfied <- ifelse(knn_test_pred == "satisfied",
                              knn_test_prob_raw, 1 - knn_test_prob_raw)

# --- ROC curve on test set ---
roc_test <- roc(te_y_final, prob_test_satisfied, levels = c("neutral or dissatisfied", "satisfied"))

cat("=== Test-Set ROC AUC ===\n")
cat(sprintf("AUC = %.4f\n", auc(roc_test)))

ggroc(roc_test, legacy.axes = TRUE, color = "#3498DB", linewidth = 1.4) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.55, y = 0.30,
           label = sprintf("AUC = %.4f", auc(roc_test)),
           size = 5.5, fontface = "bold", color = "#2C3E50") +
  labs(title = "ROC Curve \u2014 Final Model: KNN (k = 15, BIC Predictors) on Held-Out Test Set",
       
       x = "False Positive Rate (1 \u2212 Specificity)",
       y = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)") +
  theme_report()
```

## Confusion Matrices and Metrics

We evaluate at both the **default threshold (t = 0.50)** and the **optimised threshold (t = 0.34)** from the validation sweep.

```{r final-confusion-matrices}
# --- Helper: generate confusion matrix and key metrics ---
eval_at_threshold <- function(probs, true_labels, threshold, label) {
  pred_class <- factor(
    ifelse(probs >= threshold, "satisfied", "neutral or dissatisfied"),
    levels = levels(true_labels)
  )
  cm <- confusionMatrix(pred_class, true_labels, positive = "satisfied")

  cat("\n====================================================\n")
  cat(sprintf("  %s (threshold = %.2f)\n", label, threshold))
  cat("====================================================\n")
  print(cm$table)
  cat("\n")
  cat(sprintf("  Accuracy    = %.4f\n", cm$overall["Accuracy"]))
  cat(sprintf("  Sensitivity = %.4f  (Recall)\n", cm$byClass["Sensitivity"]))
  cat(sprintf("  Specificity = %.4f\n", cm$byClass["Specificity"]))
  cat(sprintf("  Precision   = %.4f\n", cm$byClass["Pos Pred Value"]))

  prec <- cm$byClass["Pos Pred Value"]
  rec  <- cm$byClass["Sensitivity"]
  f1   <- ifelse(is.na(prec) | is.na(rec) | (prec + rec == 0), 0,
                 2 * prec * rec / (prec + rec))
  cat(sprintf("  F1 Score    = %.4f\n", f1))

  invisible(list(cm = cm, f1 = f1, pred_class = pred_class))
}

res_050 <- eval_at_threshold(prob_test_satisfied, te_y_final, 0.50, "Test Set — Default")
res_034 <- eval_at_threshold(prob_test_satisfied, te_y_final, 0.34, "Test Set — Optimised")
```

```{r cm-heatmaps, fig.height=4, fig.width=10}
# --- Side-by-side confusion matrix heatmaps ---
make_cm_plot <- function(cm_obj, title_str) {
  cm_df <- as.data.frame(cm_obj$table)
  colnames(cm_df) <- c("Predicted", "Actual", "Freq")
  ggplot(cm_df, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted, fill = Freq)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1.5) +
    geom_text(aes(label = format(Freq, big.mark = ",")),
              size = 5.5, fontface = "bold") +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F7F7F7", high = "#3498DB") +
    labs(title = title_str, x = "Actual Class", y = "Predicted Class") +
    theme_report(base_size = 12) +
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold"))
}

p_cm1 <- make_cm_plot(res_050$cm, "Default Threshold (t = 0.50)")
p_cm2 <- make_cm_plot(res_034$cm, "Optimised Threshold (t = 0.34)")
gridExtra::grid.arrange(p_cm1, p_cm2, ncol = 2,
                        top = grid::textGrob("Confusion Matrices — KNN (k = 15) on Test Set",
                                             gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold")))
```

## Misclassification Analysis: Inspecting the 15 Nearest Neighbours

To understand *why* the model misclassifies certain observations, we sample 6 misclassified test observations (3 false positives, 3 false negatives under the optimised threshold) and examine the composition and feature statistics of their 15 nearest neighbours in the training set.

```{r}
install.packages("FNN")  # For get.knnx()
```

```{r misclass-analysis}
# --- Identify misclassified observations at t = 0.34 ---
pred_class_034 <- factor(
  ifelse(prob_test_satisfied >= 0.34, "satisfied", "neutral or dissatisfied"),
  levels = levels(te_y_final)
)

misclass_idx <- which(pred_class_034 != te_y_final)
fp_idx <- misclass_idx[te_y_final[misclass_idx] == "neutral or dissatisfied"]
fn_idx <- misclass_idx[te_y_final[misclass_idx] == "satisfied"]

cat(sprintf("Total misclassified at t = 0.34: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            length(misclass_idx), length(te_y_final),
            100 * length(misclass_idx) / length(te_y_final)))
cat(sprintf("  False positives: %d\n", length(fp_idx)))
cat(sprintf("  False negatives: %d\n", length(fn_idx)))

# --- Sample 3 FP and 3 FN ---
set.seed(123)
fp_sample <- sample(fp_idx, min(3, length(fp_idx)))
fn_sample <- sample(fn_idx, min(3, length(fn_idx)))
sample_idx <- c(fp_sample, fn_sample)

# --- For each sampled observation, find its 15 nearest neighbours in training set ---
# We use Euclidean distance on the baked (preprocessed) feature space
library(FNN)

nn_result <- get.knnx(as.matrix(tr_x_final), as.matrix(te_x_final[sample_idx, ]), k = 15)

# --- Build a summary for each misclassified observation ---
# Map baked column names back for interpretability
baked_colnames <- colnames(tr_x_final)

# For each sampled misclassified point, gather statistics
misclass_report <- list()
for (i in seq_along(sample_idx)) {
  obs_i <- sample_idx[i]
  nn_indices <- nn_result$nn.index[i, ]
  nn_labels  <- tr_y_final[nn_indices]

  # Vote proportions
  n_sat <- sum(nn_labels == "satisfied")
  n_dis <- sum(nn_labels == "neutral or dissatisfied")

  # Features of the test observation (original scale, before baking)
  obs_original <- test_holdout[obs_i, ]

  # Neighbourhood feature statistics (baked scale)
  nn_features <- tr_x_final[nn_indices, ]

  # The test observation features (baked scale)
  obs_baked <- te_x_final[obs_i, ]

  error_type <- ifelse(te_y_final[obs_i] == "neutral or dissatisfied", "FP", "FN")

  misclass_report[[i]] <- list(
    index = obs_i,
    error_type = error_type,
    true_label = as.character(te_y_final[obs_i]),
    predicted_prob = prob_test_satisfied[obs_i],
    nn_satisfied = n_sat,
    nn_dissatisfied = n_dis,
    obs_original = obs_original,
    obs_baked = obs_baked,
    nn_features = nn_features,
    nn_labels = nn_labels
  )
}

# --- Print summary table ---
cat("\n=== Misclassified Observations Summary ===\n\n")
for (r in misclass_report) {
  cat(sprintf("--- Obs #%d | Type: %s | True: %s | P(satisfied) = %.3f ---\n",
              r$index, r$error_type, r$true_label, r$predicted_prob))
  cat(sprintf("    Neighbours: %d satisfied, %d dissatisfied (out of 15)\n",
              r$nn_satisfied, r$nn_dissatisfied))

  # Show key original features
  obs <- r$obs_original
  cat(sprintf("    Class: %s | Travel: %s | Customer: %s | Age: %s\n",
              obs$class, obs$type_of_travel, obs$customer_type, obs$age))
  cat(sprintf("    Flight dist: %s | Dep delay: %s | Arr delay: %s\n",
              obs$flight_distance, obs$departure_delay_in_minutes,
              obs$arrival_delay_in_minutes))

  # Key service ratings
  key_ratings <- c("online_boarding", "inflight_wifi_service",
                   "inflight_entertainment", "seat_comfort",
                   "on_board_service", "leg_room_service")
  rating_vals <- sapply(key_ratings, function(v) as.character(obs[[v]]))
  cat(sprintf("    Key ratings: %s\n",
              paste(paste0(key_ratings, "=", rating_vals), collapse = ", ")))
  cat("\n")
}
```

```{r neighbour-comparison, fig.height=8, fig.width=11}
# --- Compare each misclassified observation to its neighbourhood ---
# For each sampled obs, show how its key features compare to the
# mean of its "satisfied" neighbours vs "dissatisfied" neighbours

key_features_baked <- c("online_boarding", "inflight_wifi_service",
                        "inflight_entertainment", "seat_comfort",
                        "on_board_service", "leg_room_service",
                        "checkin_service", "cleanliness")
# Keep only features that exist in the baked column names
key_features_baked <- intersect(key_features_baked, baked_colnames)

comparison_rows <- list()
for (r in misclass_report) {
  nn_sat <- r$nn_features[r$nn_labels == "satisfied", key_features_baked, drop = FALSE]
  nn_dis <- r$nn_features[r$nn_labels != "satisfied", key_features_baked, drop = FALSE]

  obs_vals <- as.numeric(r$obs_baked[, key_features_baked])

  label_str <- sprintf("Obs #%d (%s)", r$index, r$error_type)

  comparison_rows[[length(comparison_rows) + 1]] <- tibble(
    Observation = label_str,
    Feature = key_features_baked,
    Test_Obs = obs_vals,
    Mean_Sat_NN  = if (nrow(nn_sat) > 0) colMeans(nn_sat) else rep(NA, length(key_features_baked)),
    Mean_Dis_NN  = if (nrow(nn_dis) > 0) colMeans(nn_dis) else rep(NA, length(key_features_baked))
  )
}
comp_df <- bind_rows(comparison_rows)

# Reshape for plotting
comp_long <- comp_df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Test_Obs, Mean_Sat_NN, Mean_Dis_NN),
               names_to = "Source", values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(Source = dplyr::recode(Source,
    "Test_Obs" = "Misclassified Obs",
    "Mean_Sat_NN" = "Satisfied Neighbours (mean)",
    "Mean_Dis_NN" = "Dissatisfied Neighbours (mean)"
  ))

ggplot(comp_long, aes(x = Feature, y = Value, fill = Source)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), width = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ Observation, ncol = 2, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Misclassified Obs" = "#2C3E50",
                                "Satisfied Neighbours (mean)" = "#3498DB",
                                "Dissatisfied Neighbours (mean)" = "#E74C3C")) +
  labs(
    title = "Misclassified Observations vs. Their 15 Nearest Neighbours",
    subtitle = "Key service ratings (scaled 0\u20131); grouped bars compare the test observation with neighbourhood means by class",
    x = NULL, y = "Feature Value (min-max scaled)",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_report(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1, size = 9),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10)
  )
```

### Interpretation of Misclassifications

```{r misclass-interpretation}
# --- Quantify how "borderline" the neighbourhood is for each misclassified obs ---
cat("=== Neighbourhood Composition & Margin ===\n\n")
cat(sprintf("%-18s %-5s %-15s %-8s %-8s %-10s\n",
            "Observation", "Type", "True Label", "Sat NN", "Dis NN", "Margin"))
cat(paste(rep("-", 72), collapse = ""), "\n")
for (r in misclass_report) {
  margin <- abs(r$nn_satisfied - r$nn_dissatisfied) / 15
  cat(sprintf("Obs #%-12d %-5s %-15s %-8d %-8d %.3f\n",
              r$index, r$error_type, r$true_label,
              r$nn_satisfied, r$nn_dissatisfied, margin))
}

cat("\nObservations with a low margin (close to 0) sit near the decision boundary,\n")
cat("where the 15 neighbours are nearly evenly split between the two classes.\n")
cat("These are inherently ambiguous cases where the features do not clearly\n")
cat("separate satisfied from dissatisfied passengers.\n")
```

## Final Model Summary

```{r final-summary}
cat("================================================================\n")
cat("  FINAL MODEL SELECTION SUMMARY\n")
cat("================================================================\n\n")
cat("  Model              : KNN (k = 15)\n")
cat("  Data Version       : BIC-reduced (19 predictors from forward stepwise)\n")
cat("  Preprocessing      : Linear imputation, z-score + min-max scaling, dummies\n")
cat(sprintf("  CV AUC (10-fold)   : 0.9777 ± SD\n"))
cat(sprintf("  CV F1 (10-fold)    : 0.9118 ± SD\n"))
cat(sprintf("  Optimised Threshold: 0.34 (from validation set F1 sweep)\n"))
cat(sprintf("  Test AUC           : %.4f\n", auc(roc_test)))
cat(sprintf("  Test F1 (t=0.50)   : %.4f\n", res_050$f1))
cat(sprintf("  Test F1 (t=0.34)   : %.4f\n", res_034$f1))
cat("================================================================\n")
```

KNN with $k = 15$ on the BIC-reduced predictor set was selected as the final model based on a systematic comparison across five classification methods (Logistic Regression, LDA, QDA, KNN, Naive Bayes), three predictor configurations (Full, BIC-reduced, PCA-transformed), and additional exploration of penalised logistic regression (Ridge, Lasso) and Generalized Additive Models (GAMs). The model achieved the highest 10-fold cross-validated AUC (0.9777) among all configurations, substantially outperforming the parametric methods. This performance gap is consistent with the ISLR discussion of the bias-variance trade-off (Ch. 2): KNN's flexibility enables it to capture complex, potentially nonlinear decision boundaries in the feature space that linear methods cannot represent, and the large training set ($n \approx 100{,}000$) mitigates the variance penalty typically associated with nonparametric approaches.

The BIC-reduced predictor set (19 variables from forward stepwise selection) proved at least as effective as the full model across all methods, confirming that the four dropped predictors (`gate_location`, `food_and_drink`, `gender`, `flight_distance`) contribute negligible discriminative power. This convergence was independently corroborated by the LDA scaling coefficients, where these same variables received the small absolute 'weights'.

Threshold tuning on the validation set shifted the optimal decision boundary from the default $t = 0.50$ to $t = 0.34$, reflecting the moderate class imbalance (\$\sim\$57% dissatisfied) and the asymmetric cost structure implicit in the F1 metric, which equally weights precision and recall.

The misclassification analysis reveals that errors tend to occur in **boundary regions** of the feature space where the 15 nearest neighbours contain a near-even mix of both classes. These are passengers whose service ratings and travel characteristics place them in the overlap between the satisfied and dissatisfied clusters — for example, a business traveller who gave high ratings on most service dimensions but low ratings on wifi and online boarding, or a personal traveller with moderate ratings across the board. The low margin (close-to-even neighbour splits) for these observations confirms that they are genuinely ambiguous cases lying near the Bayes decision boundary, rather than systematic model failures. This pattern is consistent with the irreducible error component emphasised in ISLR (Section 2.2): no classifier can correctly separate observations that are intrinsically indistinguishable in the available feature space.

The comparison of confusion matrices at t = 0.50 vs t = 0.34 shows that the lower threshold increases sensitivity (catching more truly satisfied passengers) at a modest cost to precision. This trade-off is appropriate if the business priority is to avoid missing satisfied passengers, whereas t = 0.50 would be preferred if false positives (incorrectly predicting satisfaction) are more costly and a more conservative classification rule is desired.
